page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 6.0 GB per core. It utilizes KVM virtualization and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, falls into the bottom 10%, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. However, multi-core execution, including Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU tests, reaches average tiers. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average, but large-block uncached read and write operations are weak, landing in the bottom 25%. Database operations and static web serving throughput perform at average levels.

The shared CPU model represents a tradeoff, offering a cost-effective resource profile for workloads that do not require sustained, high-frequency single-core performance. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis databases, and moderate background processing tasks. It is less suitable for heavy single-threaded computations or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance drops significantly.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 server featuring 8 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with a shared CPU allocation model and a 2.0 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB per core, and runs on the KVM hypervisor. The server does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU and memory performance, though single-core execution and uncached large-block memory bandwidth are weak. This resource profile offers a cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations that can tolerate shared CPU limits. It is less suited for intensive single-threaded tasks or large-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 48 GB RAM. It offers balanced multi-core performance for web serving and databases under a shared resource model.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-48GB features 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, offering cost-effective multi-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A shared-core x86 server offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for cost-effective multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs with 2.0 GHz clock speed
48.0 GB of system memory providing 6.0 GB per core
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization without bundled local storage
Average multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving performance
Suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis, and general-purpose workloads
